verdict.answer_first: B states the core answer immediately: "Yes — the second comparison is the real problem"; A is direct too but starts softer with "A couple of things worth flagging".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more clearly to a concrete fix, especially "make it `return reject(...)`" and then gives one cleaned-up version; A offers alternatives and ends with "Want me to adjust...".
verdict.fabrication: B slightly speculates with "If this is a Promise's `reject`, that's almost certainly a bug"; A has none beyond context-qualified possibilities.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the corrected condition plus the control-flow fix: "return reject();".
